claim: Replacement of OCT4 and SOX2 genes with mesendoderm-related genes has the ability to reprogram human cells.

result.claim: Replacement of OCT4 and SOX2 genes with mesendoderm-related genes has the ability to reprogram human cells.
result.reasoning: The search for propositions and chunks similar to the claim did not yield any direct evidence regarding the replacement of OCT4 and SOX2 genes with mesendoderm-related genes for reprogramming human cells. While some results discuss the general concept of reprogramming human somatic cells into induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) using genes like OCT4 and SOX2 (e.g., Paper ID: 2002182, chunk_17713), and others mention mesoderm-derived lineages (e.g., Paper ID: 4427392), there is no information about the specific gene replacement described in the claim. Therefore, there is insufficient evidence to support or refute the claim.

evidence.text: All three Tet genes were deleted in the MEFs.
evidence.source: Tet and TDG mediate DNA demethylation essential for mesenchymal-to-epithelial transition in somatic cell reprogramming.

evidence.text: The CHEK2 gene activates DNA repair.
evidence.source: Linkage Disequilibrium Mapping of       CHEK2: Common Variation and Breast Cancer Risk

evidence.text: Neural progenitor cells can be reprogrammed with fewer genetic manipulations than previously reported somatic cells.
evidence.source: A Combined Chemical and Genetic Approach for the Generation of Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells

evidence.text: The stem cell field has experienced recent breakthroughs in using viral transduction of a few genes for the reprogramming of both mouse and human somatic cells into induced pluripotent stem (iPS) cells.
evidence.source: A Combined Chemical and Genetic Approach for the Generation of Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells

evidence.text: The authors generated mice with a conditional BCL-2 gene.
evidence.source: Antiapoptotic BCL-2 is required for maintenance of a model leukemia.

evidence.text: The CHEK2 gene promotes cell cycle arrest.
evidence.source: Linkage Disequilibrium Mapping of       CHEK2: Common Variation and Breast Cancer Risk

evidence.text: Precardiogenic mesoderm is initially derived from anterior lateral plate mesoderm.
evidence.source: Yin-Yang 1, a New Player in Early Heart Development

evidence.text: c-Myc is one of the four genes originally thought to be required for iPS cell generation.
evidence.source: A Combined Chemical and Genetic Approach for the Generation of Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells

evidence.text: Differences in MCF-7 cells were traced back to genetic heterogeneity using Comparative Genomic Hybridization (CGH).
evidence.source: Genetic variability in a frozen batch of MCF-7 cells invisible in routine authentication affecting cell function

evidence.text: 16S rRNA genes were amplified, cloned, and transformed into Escherichia coli.

evidence.text: MSC obtained from different human tissues have tissue repair and immunomodulatory properties.

evidence.text: Multimerization of GUS is responsible for mediating a COP phenotype on fusion to CCT1.

evidence.text: NVP-BEZ235 reversed the hyperactivation of the PI3K/mTOR pathway caused by the E545K and H1047R mutations.
evidence.source: NVP-BEZ235, a dual PI3K/mTOR inhibitor, prevents PI3K signaling and inhibits the growth of cancer cells with activating PI3K mutations.

evidence.text: Multimerization of GUS is responsible and required for mediating a COP phenotype on fusion to CCT1.
evidence.source: N-Terminal Domain–Mediated Homodimerization Is Required for Photoreceptor Activity of Arabidopsis

evidence.text: EZH2(Y646F) is the most common somatic Ezh2 gain-of-function mutation in humans.
evidence.source: An oncogenic Ezh2 mutation induces tumors through global redistribution of histone 3 lysine 27 trimethylation

evidence.text: Female iPS cells showed reactivation of a somatically silenced X chromosome.
evidence.source: Directly reprogrammed fibroblasts show global epigenetic remodeling and widespread tissue contribution.

evidence.text: LSD1 removed transcription-promoting methylation of H3K4.

evidence.text: Converting ApoE4 to ApoE3 by gene editing rescued the phenotypes of the neurons.

evidence.text: The reprogramming efficiency is reduced in the absence of c-Myc.

evidence.text: Golli protein is an alternatively spliced product of the myelin basic protein gene.

evidence.text: YY1 is a known repressor of sarcomeric gene expression.

evidence.text: Catalytically active Tet and TDG restores reprogramming in the knockout MEFs.

evidence.text: Tet-deficient MEFs cannot be reprogrammed into induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) in this study.

evidence.text: The reprogramming of MEFs deficient in TDG is impaired.

evidence.text: Maherali et al., 2007; Meissner et al., 2007; Nakagawa et al., 2008; Okita et al., 2007; Takahashi et al., 2007; Takahashi and Yamanaka, 2006; Wernig et al., 2007, 2008; Yu et al., 2007 are authors who have contributed to recent breakthroughs in the use of viral transduction for cellular reprogramming.

evidence.text: Elevated chromosome breakage correlated with a significant increase in the amount of neuronal cell death in Ku86(-/-) SOD1 transgenic embryos over that seen in Ku86(-/-) embryos.

evidence.text: Inhibition of MET in GBM mouse models blocks mesenchymal transition and invasion provoked by VEGF ablation.
evidence.source: VEGF inhibits tumor cell invasion and mesenchymal transition through a MET/VEGFR2 complex.

evidence.text: Depleting miR-27/24 in mouse embryonic fibroblasts can promote somatic cell reprogramming.
evidence.source: Functional screen reveals essential roles of miR-27a/24 in differentiation of embryonic stem cells
